item.title: DDA review is a right call says ACT NOW!
item.description: [Act Now PNG] There is clearly a huge transparency and accountability gap that still exists within DDAs. The call by the Constitutional Law Reform Commission to review DDA law is a right call.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: Eight tips for creating a culture of innovation in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Peter Williams has been advising businesses on innovation for a long time as part of his role as Chief Edge Officer at Deloitte’s Centre for the Edge in Australia. He shared his top tips for creating a successful culture of innovation at the recent 2026 Innovation PNG Awards in Port Moresby. The pos

item.title: Protecting Pacific Taro
item.description: [Education News PNG] USP Researcher Tackles Climate Challenges As the world marks World Rural Development Day on 6 July, attention turns to the vital role rural communities play The post Protecting Pacific Taro first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: Newmont PNG expands trade training with national accreditation
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Newmont PNG has reached another milestone in workforce development after its Lihir Technical Training Centre secured national accreditation to conduct onsite Belt Splicing trade testing, giving more Papua New Guineans access to recognised trade qualifications without leaving the mine site. Newmont P

item.title: Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea faces significant gold smuggling issues, undermining its economy and reducing revenue for essential services. Efforts are being made to enhance monitoring and disrupt smuggling networks for better resource management. The post Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAW

These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG PM extends congratulations to US on 250 years of independence
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has sent his congratulations to United States President Donald J. Trump, his administration and the American people following celebrations marking 250 years of American independence on July 4. The Prime Minister described the milestone as a significant mo

item.title: International Partners to Help Bolster Election Security, Says Manning
item.description: [Post Courier] International partners will play a supporting role in helping Papua New Guinea deliver a safe and secure 2027 National General Election as police work to address capability gaps and secure the resources needed for one of the country's largest security operations. To access this post, you must purcha

item.title: Chief Inspector Kelly Sombe Appointed New Southern Highlands Provincial Police Commander
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Chief Inspector Kelly Sombe has officially been appointed as the new Provincial Police Commander (PPC) for Southern Highlands Province, effective immediately. The appointment was confirmed in an official police minute dated 30 June 2026, signed by Deputy Commissioner of Police and Chief of Regional

item.title: God is not done with you, preacher Kalan tells Worshipers
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Christians were encouraged to remain faithful through life’s hardships with the assurance that God has a purpose for every challenge, as preacher Steward Kalan reminded worshippers that “God is not done with you.” Preaching last Sabbath from Jeremiah 29:11 at Sogavare Memorial SDA in Gerehu Stage 6

item.title: Ol ami rausim planti bom long East New Britain provins
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol bom ekspet na sapot opisa bilong dispela wok i kam long Australia, PNG, New Zealand, Canada, France, Japan, UK na USA i bin stap insait long wanpela operesen we ol i rausim inap 2,200 bom bilong Wol Woa 2 long East New Britain provins long Jun dispela yia. Australia i go pas long dispela wok anin

item.title: Mining Project Sparks Fears of Environmental Disaster
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Major mining project that could discharge hundreds of millions of tonnes of waste into the sea off Papua New Guinea

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Waste not, feed more: poultry farm’s award-winning solution has wings
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A small poultry farm in Papua New Guinea’s Central Province has found a sustainable way of reducing its dependence on expensive stock feed. Its solution, which harnesses the power of a humble insect, was one of the big winners in this year’s Innovation PNG Awards. The post Waste not, feed more: poul

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: PNG Firm Brave Group Partners Australian Company to Launch New Integrity and Security Division
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Brave Group Ltd CEO Wesley Roy Papua New Guinea is set to strengthen its governance and professional standards after PNG-owned Brave Group (PNG) Ltd entered into a strategic partnership with Australia's Pinnacle Integrity Pty Ltd to establish the country's first integrated Integrity, Cultural Risk a

item.title: TVET and Universities in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's education system offers two main pathways: Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) focuses on practical skills for immediate employment, while universities emphasise theoretical knowledge and long-term career preparation. The post TVET and Universities in Papua New

item.title: Reshuffle looms for PNG Government as Marape tightens performance checks
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's Cabinet lineup is set for an overhaul, with Prime Minister James Marape signalling that a reshuffle will be announced within days. Speaking ahead of the expected Thursday or Friday announcement, the Prime Minister said the move stems from a lengthy review of how well ministers hav

item.title: Police Do Not Decide Election Winners, Manning Tells Commanders
item.description: [Post Courier] Police Commissioner David Manning has issued a strong reminder to senior police commanders that the Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary's role in the 2027 National General Election is to provide security—not determine election outcomes. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , We

item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister James Marape has announced that he will undertake a Cabinet reshuffle and portfolio restructuring later this week as part of an effort to make sure Government delivers on its commitments to the people of Papua New Guinea. The Prime Minister said the reshuffle, expected on Thursday or

item.title: PM Marape promises greater benefits for Gulf through Papua LNG
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has assured Gulf Province and Papua LNG landowners they will receive greater long-term benefits from the multi-billion-Kina project, saying negotiations with developers are close to delivering a stronger deal for Papua New Guinea. Speaking during the opening of the Ihu M

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.description: [Act Now PNG] Constitutional Law Reform Commission (CLRC) will review the laws on DDA

item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] AAP: By Tess Ikonomou Australia can now count on Fiji as an ally as Anthony Albanese declares a diplomatic triumph in sealing two new major agreements with Suva. Australia and Fiji have sealed a new defence alliance, marking a major diplomatic win for Anthony Albanese against China for influence in

item.title: PM Marape, Kikori MP launch new Market, K315 million Development Package for Kikori
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape and local Kikori MP, Soroi Marepo Eoe launched more than K315 million in infrastructure projects and officially opened the new Ihu Market last Friday. The launch was described as a major investment boost for developments in Kikori District, Gulf province. Related news: h

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: PNG and the Failure of EUDR Benchmarking: When “Low Risk” Ignores Reality
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Papua New Guinea’s designation as “low risk” in the EUDR may now be one of the clearest examples of the disconnect with reality.

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] SP Brewery’s beers have been sold in PNG since 1952, almost a quarter-century before independence. item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Minister for National Emergency, Hon. Dr Billy Joseph, has condemned the circulation of old photographs on fake social media accounts, describing them as a deliberate attempt to mislead the public into believing he continues to represent Papua New Guinea at international Defence engagements. item.title: Westpac PNG forecasts solid growth as PNG Economy remains resilient
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Papua New Guinea’s economy has remained resilient despite global uncertainty and rising energy prices, according to a commercial bank report. item.description: [The PNG Sun] Minister for Commerce and Industry Win Daki has been stripped of his ministerial portfolio pending an independent investigation into the Government’s K200 million Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Fund, with Finance Minister Thomas Opa taking over the ministry in addition to his existing responsibil

item.title: Dei bai-ileksen suruk bek tupela de

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘We’re challenging ourselves and our competitors to do better’: Kina Bank’s CEO on the push for innovation in PNG’s finance sector
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Kina Bank has been leading the way in bringing digital banking products to Papua New Guineans, most recently with the launch of the Pei Beta digital wallet earlier this year. Its CEO Ivan Vidovich shared his bank’s vision on digital payments and innovation in an on-stage interview at the Innovation

item.title: PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae
item.description: [Education News PNG] Thirty-one cadets from the PNG Joint Forces Academy successfully completed a major field training exercise in Lae this week as part of their final military The post PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary El Niño and Prolonged Drought
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG braces for an extraordinary dry season with looming water shortages and crop stress. Districts ordered to map local water sources. item.title: PNG Ready to Share Mining Experience as Solomon Islands Plans Resource Growth
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea has pledged to assist Solomon Islands develop a stronger mining industry by sharing its experience in resource governance and encouraging PNG companies to invest in future mining ventures across the neighbouring Melanesian nation. PNG Ready to Share Mining Experience as Solomon Isla

item.title: The Story of the Meri Blouse
item.description: [Pawa TV] A Symbol of PNG Identity. The meri blouse, locally also called kolos in Tok Pisin, is one of the most recognisable garments associated with women in Papua New Guinea (PNG). Featuring a loose, comfortable fit often crafted from colourful fabric with bold motifs, it serves not just as clothing but as

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Defence Force Personnel Complete Trade Training Under Australia Partnership
item.description: [PNG Facts] Thirty-five members of the PNG Defence Force (PNGDF) have graduated from an eight-week vocational training program, equipping them with practical automotive and carpentry skills to strengthen the organisation's technical workforce. PNG Defence Force Personnel Complete Trade Training Under Australia

item.title: Governor Powi Welcomes New Electoral Commissioner
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] SOUTHERN Highlands Governor William Powi, MP, has welcomed the National Government’s appointment of Dr Nicole Haley as Papua New Guinea’s new Electoral Commissioner, describing it as a historic and courageous decision that will strengthen public confidence in the country’s democratic institutions an

item.title: PM Marape arrives in Ihu to launch K316 million infrastructure programme
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape this morning arrived in Ihu, Kikori District, to launch more than K316 million worth of infrastructure and community projects aimed at improving transport, markets and public services across East and West Ihu Local Level Governments. The launches are being held under the

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: PM Marape Congratulates United States on 250th Anniversary of Independence
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister James Marape has extended warm congratulations to President Donald J. Trump, the Government and the American people as they celebrate the 250th Anniversary of the Independence of the United States of America today. Prime Minister Marape said the occasion marked a historic milestone no

item.title: Office of Censorship destroys 645 prohibited items
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Office of Censorship has destroyed 645 prohibited items confiscated during enforcement operations across Papua New Guinea in 2025, reinforcing its commitment to enforcing censorship laws and protecting the public from illegal imports. item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The winners of Papua New Guinea’s major awards for innovation were announced at a special breakfast on 3 June at the Hilton Hotel in Port Moresby. item.description: [The PNG Sun] The University of Papua New Guinea is preparing to welcome students back for Semester Two of the 2026 academic year, with registration underway ahead of classes beginning on Monday, July 6. item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: PNG Minister Daki Faces Renewed Resignation Calls Over K200 Million SME Scheme
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Commerce and Industry Minister Win Bakri Daki is facing renewed pressure to resign after Opposition Leader James Nomane intensified calls for an independent investigation into the Government's K200 million Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) funding scheme, arguing that key questions
